_comment: Coverage map. Maps each SEEDED SPEC.md § Validation code to the EXPECTED fixture(s) that fire it. The SPEC table has 50 codes (v0.2 plus the additive timestamp-missing codes, plus DB_MD_SCHEMA_FIELD for malformed `## Schemas` field declarations and FM_UNREADABLE for unreadable content files, plus FM_BAD_META_TYPE for an out-of-enum records-only `meta-type`, plus the five ASSET_* asset-manifest codes, plus the v0.4 FM_BAD_ID warning for a structurally unusable `id`, plus the FM_IN_BODY warning for a body that opens with a second frontmatter block); this corpus seeds 39 and explicitly leaves FM_MISSING_CREATED / FM_MISSING_UPDATED / FM_BAD_META_TYPE / FM_BAD_ID / FM_IN_BODY / FM_UNREADABLE plus the five ASSET_* codes uncovered because the designed malformed files exercise bad timestamps and missing type/summary instead, every seeded records file carries a valid (or absent, defaulting to fact) meta-type, every explicit id in the corpus is a legal opaque token (v0.4 keeps hand-authored slug ids silent by design), no imported file leaves a second frontmatter block in its body (FM_IN_BODY is exercised by the validate.rs unit tests instead), and corpus-b has no binary-asset fixtures. The e2e_corpus_b.rs test asserts (1) coverage keys are a subset of the SPEC table (no invented codes) and (2) `uncovered_spec_codes` exactly equals SPEC-codes minus seeded-codes (both directions), so this file cannot drift out of sync with the SPEC table. The four DB_MD_* codes are seeded by the bad-db-md/ sub-store (EXPECTED/bad-db-md.json) — the three identity-contract codes plus DB_MD_SCHEMA_FIELD via its `### expense` unique-key-on-non-required-fields schema. DUP_UNIQUE_KEY is seeded by the six dup-pair fixtures via this store's DB.md `unique:` directives; v0.2 replaced the six type-specific DUP_* codes and LAYER_TYPE_MISMATCH with it. The wiki/ layer was removed: WIKI_LINK_AMBIGUOUS / WIKI_LINK_FLOW_FORM_LIST / POLICY_IGNORED_TYPE_DERIVED / INDEX_ORPHAN are now seeded by records/ fixtures (records/synthesis/*, records/people/, records/misc/ambiguous-link.md). INDEX_JSONL_DESYNC is grouped under plan_extensions for provenance but counts as a seeded SPEC code in the coverage math.
